Huge love to both Van and Taira. That was a tremendous fight. We got to see every aspect of MMA at an absurdly high level. That's what it's all about
Joshua Van is such an incredible fighter. He deserves better than this era of MMA
Van remembers the body. And that's it. Taira folds. A couple body shots! All it took
Van crushing Taira with the retreating jab now. Taira fighting desperately, which means he's running into these shots HARD
Van's jab is just a thing of beauty.
Taira lands a hard jab and a nice 3-2 to open up round 5. He's got to keep throwing aggressively if he wants to stop Van running him over again
Van lands two more sickening jabs and the round ends. Toughest round of the fight to score. Incredible that Taira is even still competing at this point
Van lands a sick knee off the quarter nelson
But Van looks perfectly calm back on the feet. He's fully recovered. Killing Taira with the jab again
The moment Taira postures Van explodes. Taira slaps on a triangle! But Van has his hand in. Looks to be alright, and he's out. This fight has everything
This isn't even the same sport as Volkov vs Waldo
Calf kick from Taira. Van backpedals, Taira steps in with the uppercut! But Van counters. taira shoots, gets the takedown. WHAT A FIGHT
Taira is a tough bastard. Time to find out how much Van spent trying to kill him
And Taira gets the takedown. How wild will it be if Van gassed trying to finish him and now Taira overwhelms him

Van has the distance so dialed in, and Taira can't get a scrap of initiative going. Scrap that: Taira bites down and charges forward with a combination. now wrestling Van against the fence. Tough dude
Van trying to make a point with the choke finish. But Taira is defending. Van smashes his face a few times, sinks in the choke deeper. Now he's got Taira fleeing a ground exchange. This fight is over
UUUUGGGHHH the uppercut from Van. Everything coming off the jab. Speaking of which, JAB KNOCKDOWN! Taira is done. Fight over
Taira shoots into a knee! I told you Van would not get suckered by that same shot all night. I love this man
Cross counter from Van. Lead uppercut follows, lands flush. Taira forcing the takedowns now. Van corners him, lands a thudding right over the top. Snapping Taira's head back with the jab again
Ugh Taira shoots straight into a STIFF jab. Van sticking it in his chest as he walks him down
Tatsuro Taira was straight up knocked out there. One more clean shot and the ref would have had no choice. Saved by the bell
This is MMA baby
Joshua Van!
If Taira can't get anything going on the ground here then this is a definite Van round. And he lets Van up chasing the D'arce OOOOOOH HUGE KNOCKDOWN BY VAN. BIG RIGHT HAND
Taira resorting to some genuine lay & pray because every time he postures up even slightly Van creates a scramble
Uh oh. Van starting to land the right hand repeatedly. Taira collapsing on the feet. Losing control of the distance. But here comes the double leg and he's in full mount. This is fantastic
Taira's shot looks great tonight, but Van sprawls beautifully and defends the first one of round 2 (so far). Sick knee by Taira on the break!
Rough start, but Van got up quicker and quicker each time he got taken down, and at some point he's gonna catch Taira's timing off the pressure
Taira's takedowns are on point tonight. Sensational trip to return Van to the ground. Van scrambling brilliantly though
Van hip heists and gets back to his feet with 50 seconds to go in r1. Grimy fight so far
Van pressuring behind a stiff jab. Gained a little momentum there but Taira times another beautiful double leg. Van can get back up but it may take the rest of the round
Like Taira got one through, but it's not like Van doesn't have an answer. It's nice to have faith in a fighter's technique and thought process
One thing I like about Joshua Van is knowing that (provided he survives on the ground here) he's not going to just let this keep happening the same way the rest of the fight

Trappings bring meaning, meaning brings talent, talent brings strategy and money, and focus. Narrative grows because we give the results meaning. Sport without meaning is fun to play, but boring to watch.

That's what the UFC continually tries to get away with offering. Which is why they keep all the rest of the stuff as dull as possible.

If that's all MMA ever was, though, I can't see sticking with it. Be like if baseball didn't have a world series everyone was playing toward.

@CNick20152 I didn't make the comparison. The interviewer did. And Dana White didn't say "hey look, we pay better than our competition" he said "we aren't signing real athletes so we aren't paying them real wages."
@clutchdagger11 Yes. Literally every pro sport is signing athletes to contracts based on potential and not guaranteed production. They just scout better and pay more.

@liefambagtskat @AikidoBender It’s also a consequence of a long term refusal to scout talent or develop it with any meaningful intent. Cheaper and easier to simply sign everyone and see what shakes out. But it absolutely makes the product worse.
@liefambagtskat @AikidoBender They’ve been putting on the same number of cards a year for more than a decade. The slop era started with COVID and never stopped.

I remember doing the math on the UFC’s big new Reebok deal when they were making all their NFL claims and finding out that it was more or less in line with what companies were paying the MLS for jersey rights.
This promotion used to tell people it was the new NFL, that it would be bigger than soccer. Now it’s begging people not to hold it to the WNBA’s pay scale.

Media companies will keep falling for this kind of stuff because they don't see a path to clear long term profitability. So it's either "let it die very slowly" or "do something big and stupid and watch it die fast."

Feels like the long arc on all internet stuff is that it generates no money unless you get people to subscribe directly, which is incredibly hard to do. Advertising, clicks, views, all of it adds up to nada and big media companies were built on advertising not subscribing.
All told, I'd say there's one fight on the whole card that feels meaningfully impactful on the careers of the men in it and it's a BW prelim bout absolutely nobody cares about.
Such an 'empty calories' card from the UFC. Song and Figgy are both elite BWs capable of being in meaningful fights, but this fight specifically feels like it means absolutely nothing.

Drag hit its second-lowest point since 2020 on June 18 (.323), but bounced up toward early May levels the past two days (.344); a difference of +/- 8 feet in expected barrel distance

Important to note, fluctuations like this are very normal! .330 on June 7th and .356 on June 8th are among the high and low days for the full season, and happened consecutively

Main point is that we have not seen a cluster of daily drag lows in that .32 to .33 range since 2019, which could make July + August explosive

https://t.co/ygUgWrx6ky

wOBA by month to strip out noise - conclusions hold directionally

June 2026:
• Highest single-month wOBA since 2019
• Largest month-over-month wOBA gain of the Statcast era (+.018, ~3.4x a normal month)

Would genuinely value @tangotiger's read: is there an explanation for the sudden drag drop / carry spike that isn't the ball? Hard to think of anyone better to weigh in
@TheModelManiac @RotoSurgeon Really appreciate that, Byron, thank you!

tldr: It's not entirely the weather

• Gains for run scoring and barrel distance are completely normal as the weather warms from April to the summer months, but the average (4.6 ft) and prior high (6.4 ft) are well below the 2026 increase (10.2 ft)

• Warming in 2026 is essentially average compared to a 10-year sample. From April to June 2026, temps rose by 14.4 degrees, almost exactly the prior decade's average of 14.0. You can anticipate about an extra 0.3 feet of carry per increase of one degree Fahrenheit, so a 14-degree increase essentially aligns with the typical +4.6-foot gain in carry from April to June.

• The hottest warm-up in the data (2018, +18.6 degrees from April to June) produced additional carry of +6.4 feet, still well below 2026 

• In dome and roof-closed games, where there's lessened weather impact, average barrel carry typically improves minimally from April to June — about 1.4 feet and never more than three feet. In 2026, indoor parks gained 9.0 feet of average carry since late May.

• Directional differences in humidity and air pressure for indoor parks would need to be so drastically different from 2026 compared to prior years to justify that 9 ft increase that it would be a bigger anomaly than a ball change itself

On Memorial Day, barrel carry jumped ~9 feet overnight and hasn't come down - erasing five years of a deadened ball in a single week

Rico Verhoeven was NOT robbed of a century level upset. Sure, the referee may have jumped the gun. However, we would have witnessed a completely different bout in Usyk vs. Rico had it not been for open scoring. Make no mistake about it.
The @WBCBoxing  utilized an open scoring system for this fight, meaning the judges' scorecards were announced between rounds. After eight rounds, the fight was tied 76-76 on all three cards. This allowed both corners to adjust their strategies in real-time. But let's look at the bigger picture: Usyk @usykboxing  underwhelming pace was a calculated move. He could have gotten him out of there much earlier.

Instead, pacing the fight builds the drama necessary to set up a 1/2 dozen more lucrative, cross sport paydays. This is the reality of the modern crossover promotion machine. We have seen this playbook run time & time again:
🥊 Mayweather vs. McGregor: @TheNotoriousMMA  was gifted the first 3 rounds by the defensive GOAT. Floyd opened as a massive -2500 favorite but closed at just -400 because the public bought into the manufactured hype. @FloydMayweather  wasted no time overseas with  Tenshin Nasukawa.
🥊 Fury vs. Ngannou: Tyson @Tyson_Fury never stepped on the gas, got knocked down, and entirely changed the fight dynamic resulting in a Draw & another massive payday for Francis. 
🥊 Joshua vs. Ngannou & Paul: AJ wasn't going to make Fury's mistake w/ @francis_ngannou, getting him outa there exactly like he was supposed to. (Ngannou down in round one and twice in round two.) Even against Jake Paul, Joshua coasted until he decided to step on the gas late, and when he did, Jake’s jaw ended up needing to be wired.

These fighters are -2500 favorites for a reason. The skill disparity in these crossover fights is absolutely insurmountable, especially when you are talking about the top 3 Boxing heavyweights of their era.
This cycle will keep happening. It’s a rinse & repeat model for massive paydays, & from a business perspective, that’s great for everyone involved.

But when it comes to wagering, if you like money, you can't buy into underdog hype or rely on results from past crossover spectacles. Bettors often fall into trap of thinking, "Well, the underdog can win 1 in 20 times at those odds so lets take a shot."
The problem is "they don't fight 20 times. They fight once." 🍿🥯👊

Attorney Melissa Glass, who represents Mayweather, wrote to Judge Broderick today (before Broderick was to make a ruling on the request for an injunction from CSI). Here is a portion of the letter, which I have obtained: "I also write to notify the Court of an important recent development. Because Plaintiffs’ Motion remains pending without a ruling, the Mayweather-Zambidis event will no longer proceed in Athens, Greece as planned on Saturday evening.  Plaintiffs’ commencement of this action and filing of the Motion, as well as their legal threats domestically and in Greece, have effectively stopped any promotion or distribution plans for the event and halted ticket sales. Due to the fluid situation on the ground in Athens and significant damage to the commercial viability of the event, and given the time difference and imminence of its scheduled start time on June 27, the difficult decision was made to cancel the June 27 exhibition fight. Depending on the Court’s ruling, the event may be rescheduled for a later date." #boxing
